description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: South Carolina (  KARR-ə-LY-nə) is a state in the Southeastern, South Atlantic and Deep South regions of the United States.

description: private liberal arts college in Greenville, South Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Furman University is a private university in Greenville, South Carolina, United States.

description: private university in Durham, North Carolina, USA
description_wiki: Duke University is a private research university in Durham, North Carolina, United States.

description: private research university located in Pasadena, California, USA
description_wiki: The California Institute of Technology (branded as Caltech) is a private research university/institute of technology in Pasadena, California, United States.

description: fundamental theory in physics describing the properties of nature on an atomic scale
description_wiki: Quantum mechanics is the fundamental physical theory that describes the behavior of matter and of light; its unusual characteristics typically occur at and below the scale of atoms.

description: field of physics that deals with the structure and behavior of atomic nuclei
description_wiki: Nuclear physics is the field of physics that studies atomic nuclei and their constituents and interactions, in addition to the study of other forms of nuclear matter.

description: two interrelated physics theories by Albert Einstein
description_wiki: The theory of relativity comprises two physics theories by Albert Einstein: special relativity and general relativity, proposed and published in 1905 and 1915, respectively.

surface_forms: great physicists like Smythe, Zwicky, Oppenheimer, and Pauling
description: private research university located in Pasadena, California, USA
description_wiki: The California Institute of Technology (branded as Caltech) is a private research university/institute of technology in Pasadena, California, United States.

description: research and scientific development company
1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: World War II radar research operation at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description_wiki: The Radiation Laboratory (commonly called the Rad Lab) was a radar research program operating at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) during World War II. From 1940 to 1945, the Rad Lab applied new microwave technologies to develop compact radar sets for military navigation and combat.

aliases: Columbia University in the City of New York
description: private university in New York City, New York, US
description_wiki: Columbia University in the City of New York, commonly referred to as Columbia University, is a private Ivy League research university in New York City, United States.

description: office within the United States Department of the Navy
description_wiki: The Office of Naval Research (ONR) is an organization within the United States Department of the Navy responsible for the science and technology programs of the U.S. Navy and Marine Corps.

aliases: microwave amplification by stimulated emission of radiation
description: device that produces coherent electromagnetic waves through amplification by stimulated emission
description_wiki: A maser is a device that produces coherent electromagnetic waves (microwaves), through amplification by stimulated emission.

aliases: the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics
description: former country in Eurasia (1922–1991)
description_wiki: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991.

description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: American national security analysis nonprofit
description_wiki: The Institute for Defense Analyses (IDA) is an American non-profit corporation that administers three federally funded research and development centers (FFRDCs) – the Systems and Analyses Center (SAC), the Science and Technology Policy Institute (STPI), and the Center for Communications and Computing (C&C) – to assist the United States government in addressing national security issues, particularly those requiring scientific and technical expertise.

description: advising body to the U.S. President from Truman to Nixon
description_wiki: The President's Science Advisory Committee (PSAC) was created on November 21, 1957, by President of the United States Dwight D. Eisenhower, as a direct response to the Soviet launching of the Sputnik 1 and Sputnik 2 satellites.

description: private university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, USA, founded 1861
description_wiki: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) is a private research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States.

description: public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.

description: set of annual international awards, primarily 5 established in 1895 by Alfred Nobel
description_wiki: The Nobel Prizes are awards administered by the Nobel Foundation and granted in accordance with the principle of "for the greatest benefit to humankind".

description: subfield of astronomy that studies celestial objects at radio frequencies
description_wiki: Radio astronomy is a subfield of astronomy that studies celestial objects using radio waves.
